scenario: Catalyst Healthcare runs a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot worker fleet that processes 3500 jobs per minute across hospital integration flows. The configuration still uses the legacy zeebe.client.job prefix for the per-job timeout and the platform team must adopt the documented replacement key in the 8.8 properties reference to keep worker defaults centralised.
question: Which property listed in the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter reference replaces the legacy zeebe.client.job.timeout key?

options.text: The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.job.timeout key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.job.timeout under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.job.timeout key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.worker.timeout under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.job.timeout key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy zeebe.client.job.timeout key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.zeebe.job.timeout under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.

a.text: Incorrect. The 8.8 reference moves per-job timeout defaults under worker defaults; there is no camunda.client.job.timeout entry in the properties reference table.

b.text: Incorrect. The replacement key uses the .defaults. infix to mark it as a worker default; camunda.client.worker.timeout is not the documented entry.

c.text: Correct. The properties reference maps zeebe.client.job.timeout to camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout, grouping it with other job worker default settings in 8.8.

d.text: Incorrect. The replacement drops the zeebe segment; the 8.8 reference does not list a camunda.client.zeebe.job.timeout property.

explanation: In 8.8 the Spring Boot starter consolidates job worker default settings under camunda.client.worker.defaults.*; the documented replacement for zeebe.client.job.timeout is camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout.
